message:
fix(polecat): persist findings to bead before closing steps (gt-ah2k)

Polecats lose all non-code work (audits, reviews, research) on session
death because findings exist only in context — never persisted. The
checkpoint package exists but was dead code for normal work lifecycle.

Changes:
- mol-polecat-work formula v6: add "persist findings" instructions to
  implement step, require bd update --notes/--design BEFORE closing
  steps, document gt done --cleanup-status clean for report-only tasks
- polecat.md.tmpl: add "Persist Findings (Session Survival)" section
  to the rendered prime context
- polecat-CLAUDE.md: add matching section to static template

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -47,7 +47,7 @@ for step details — do NOT read this file directly.
 | Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
 | Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |"""
 formula = "mol-polecat-work"
-version = 5
+version = 6
 
 [[steps]]
 id = "load-context"
@@ -236,7 +236,25 @@ Do the actual implementation work.
 - Keep changes scoped to the assigned issue
 - Don't gold-plate or scope-creep
 
-**Commit frequently:**
+**Persist findings as you go (CRITICAL for session survival):**
+Your session can die at any time (context limit, crash, SIGKILL). Code changes
+survive in git, but analysis, findings, and decisions exist only in your context
+window. Persist them to the bead so they survive session death:
+```bash
+# After completing significant analysis or reaching conclusions:
+bd update {{issue}} --notes "Findings so far: <what you discovered>"
+# For detailed reports, use --design:
+bd update {{issue}} --design "<structured findings>"
+```
+Do this BEFORE closing molecule steps, not after. If your session dies between
+persisting and closing, the findings survive. If you close first, they're lost.
+
+**For report-only tasks** (audits, reviews, research): your findings ARE the
+deliverable. There are no code changes to commit. You MUST persist all findings
+to the bead via --notes or --design. Without this, your entire work product is
+lost when the session ends.
+
+**Commit frequently (for code tasks):**
 ```bash
 # After each logical unit of work:
 git add <files>
@@ -263,7 +281,7 @@ Problem: <what's blocking you>
 Tried: <what you've attempted>"
 ```
 
-**Exit criteria:** Implementation complete, all changes committed."""
+**Exit criteria:** Implementation complete. Code tasks: all changes committed. Report tasks: all findings persisted to bead."""
 
 [[steps]]
 id = "self-review"
@@ -401,9 +419,14 @@ Must show "nothing to commit, working tree clean".
 ```bash
 git log origin/{{base_branch}}..HEAD --oneline
 ```
-If you made changes during implementation, this MUST show at least 1 commit.
+If you made code changes during implementation, this MUST show at least 1 commit.
+
+**Report-only tasks (no code changes):** If your task was an audit, review, or
+research with no code changes, verify your findings are persisted to the bead
+(`bd show {{issue}}` — check notes/design fields). Then proceed — `gt done`
+handles the no-commit case.
 
-**Exit criteria:** All changes committed. Working tree clean."""
+**Exit criteria:** All changes committed (or findings persisted for report tasks). Working tree clean."""
 
 [[steps]]
 id = "cleanup-workspace"
@@ -460,11 +483,17 @@ bd show {{issue}}
 # Status should still be 'in_progress' (you're working on it)
 ```
 
-**2. Add completion notes:**
+**2. Add completion notes (if not already persisted during implementation):**
 ```bash
 bd update {{issue}} --notes "Implemented: <brief summary of what was done>"
 ```
 
+For report-only tasks, verify your findings are already on the bead:
+```bash
+bd show {{issue}}    # Check that --notes or --design has your findings
+```
+If not, persist them NOW — this is your last chance before `gt done`.
+
 **3. Sync beads:**
 ```bash
 bd sync
@@ -488,7 +517,11 @@ Once you run `gt done`, you're gone. The command:
 
 **Run gt done:**
 ```bash
+# For code tasks (has commits):
 gt done
+
+# For report-only tasks (no commits — audits, reviews, research):
+gt done --cleanup-status clean
 ```
 
 You should see output like:

@@ -256,6 +256,24 @@ bd mol current             # See next step
 
 When all steps are done, the molecule gets squashed automatically when you run `{{ cmd }} done`.
 
+### Persist Findings (Session Survival)
+
+Your session can die at any time (context limit, crash, SIGKILL). Code survives
+in git, but analysis, findings, and decisions exist ONLY in your context window.
+
+**Persist to the bead BEFORE closing molecule steps:**
+```bash
+bd update <issue-id> --notes "Findings: <what you discovered>"
+bd update <issue-id> --design "<structured findings>"
+```
+
+If you close a step but haven't persisted findings, and your session dies, the
+work is lost forever.
+
+**Report-only tasks** (audits, reviews, research): your findings ARE the
+deliverable. No code to commit. You MUST persist findings to the bead.
+Use `gt done --cleanup-status clean` when completing with no code changes.
+
 ## PR Workflow (for repos that use pull requests)
 
 **Applies to:** longeye and other repos that require code review via GitHub PRs

@@ -238,6 +238,24 @@ Batch-closing corrupts the timeline — it creates a lie showing all steps compl
 bd close <step-id> --reason "Implemented: <what you did>"
 ```
 
+### Persist Findings (Session Survival)
+
+Your session can die at any time. Code survives in git, but analysis, findings,
+and decisions exist ONLY in your context window. **Persist to the bead:**
+
+```bash
+# After significant analysis or conclusions:
+bd update <issue-id> --notes "Findings: <what you discovered>"
+# For detailed reports:
+bd update <issue-id> --design "<structured findings>"
+```
+
+**Do this BEFORE closing molecule steps.** If you close a step but haven't
+persisted findings, and your session dies, the work is lost forever.
+
+**Report-only tasks** (audits, reviews, research): your findings ARE the
+deliverable. No code changes to commit. You MUST persist all findings to the bead.
+
